questions.question: Ratio of boys to girls is 2:3 in a class of 50. Find number of girls.
questions.hint: The ratio 2:3 means 5 total parts. Divide 50 by 5 to find one part, then multiply by 3.

questions.question: Convert ratio 4:5 into percentage.
questions.hint: Write the ratio as 4/5 and multiply by 100.

questions.question: 30% of 150 students like sports. How many do not?
questions.hint: If 30% like sports, then 70% do not. Calculate 70% of 150.

questions.question: If 25% of a number is 75, find the number.
questions.hint: 25% is 1/4th. If 1/4th of x = 75, then x = 75 * 4.

questions.question: Out of 40 items, 10 are defective. Find percentage defective.
questions.hint: Divide defective items by total items (10/40) and multiply by 100.

questions.question: Price increases from ₹100 to ₹120. Find % increase.
questions.hint: Increase is ₹20. Divide this by original price (100) and multiply by 100.

questions.question: Price decreases from ₹200 to ₹150. Find % decrease.
questions.hint: Decrease is ₹50. Divide by original (200) and multiply by 100.

questions.question: Population grows from 1000 to 1100. Find % increase.
A number reduces by 20%. What percent remains?
questions.hint: Total percent is always 100%. Subtract the reduction.

questions.question: If salary becomes ₹2200 after 10% increase, find original salary.
questions.hint: ₹2200 represents 110% of the original salary.

questions.question: MP = ₹1000, discount = 20%. Find SP.
questions.hint: Calculate 20% of 1000 and subtract it from the Marked Price.

questions.question: SP = ₹850 after ₹150 discount. Find MP.
questions.hint: Marked Price = Selling Price + Discount.

questions.question: Find discount % if MP ₹500 and SP ₹400.
questions.hint: Discount amount is 100. (100 / 500) * 100 = 20%.

questions.question: A ₹2000 item has 10% discount. Find saving.
questions.hint: Saving is simply the 10% discount amount on ₹2000.

questions.question: If SP is ₹900 after 10% discount, find MP.
questions.hint: If discount is 10%, then 900 is 90% of the Marked Price.

questions.question: CP ₹400, SP ₹500. Find profit%.
CP ₹800, SP ₹720. Find loss%.
Find SP if CP ₹1000 with 20% profit.
questions.hint: SP = CP + 20% of CP. Or CP * 1.2.

questions.question: SP ₹900 with 10% loss. Find CP.
questions.hint: At 10% loss, SP is 90% of CP. So 900 / 0.9 = CP.

questions.question: CP ₹500 + ₹50 overhead, SP ₹660. Find profit%.
questions.hint: Total CP = 550. Profit = 660 - 550 = 110. Find percent on 550.

questions.question: Price ₹1000, GST 18%. Find total.
questions.hint: Add 18% of 1000 to the original price.

questions.question: Total ₹1180 includes 18% GST. Find original.
questions.hint: Divide the total by 1.18 to find the pre-tax price.

questions.question: Find tax amount on ₹500 at 10%.
questions.hint: Calculate 10% of 500.

questions.question: ₹220 includes 10% tax. Find base price.
questions.hint: Divide 220 by 1.10.

questions.question: Tax increases from ₹50 to ₹75. Find % increase.
Find SI on ₹1000 at 10% for 2 years.
questions.hint: Use (P * R * T) / 100.

questions.question: Find SI on ₹2000 at 5% for 3 years.
questions.hint: Multiply 2000 * 0.05 * 3.

questions.question: Find amount if SI ₹200 on ₹1000.
questions.hint: Amount = Principal + Interest.

questions.question: Find rate if SI ₹100 on ₹1000 for 1 year.
questions.hint: Rate = (SI * 100) / (P * T).

questions.question: Find time if SI ₹200, P ₹1000, R 10%.
questions.hint: Time = (SI * 100) / (P * R).

questions.question: Find amount on ₹1000 at 10% for 2 years.
questions.hint: Calculate Year 1 (1100), then add 10% of 1100 for Year 2.

questions.question: Find CI on ₹1000 at 10% for 2 years.
questions.hint: Compound Interest = Amount - Principal (1210 - 1000).

questions.question: Which grows faster: SI or CI?
questions.hint: CI earns interest on previously earned interest, SI does not.

questions.question: Find amount on ₹2000 at 5% for 2 years.
Find CI on ₹2000 at 5% for 2 years.
questions.hint: Subtract principal from final amount (2205 - 2000).

questions.question: If compounded half-yearly, how many periods in 1 year?
questions.hint: Interest is added every 6 months.

questions.question: If rate is 10% yearly, what is half-yearly rate?
questions.hint: Divide the annual rate by the number of periods (2).

questions.question: If quarterly, how many periods per year?
questions.hint: There are four 3-month segments in a year.

questions.question: Quarterly rate if annual rate is 12%?
questions.hint: Divide the annual rate (12%) by 4 periods.

questions.question: Why does CI increase faster with more compounding?
questions.hint: Interest starts earning its own interest sooner.

questions.question: Population 1000 grows 10% yearly. Find after 1 year.
questions.hint: Add 10% of 1000 to the current population.

questions.question: Value ₹1000 depreciates 10%. Find after 1 year.
questions.hint: Subtract 10% from the current value.

questions.question: Population 1000 grows 10% for 2 years.
questions.hint: Use the CI formula: 1000 * (1.1)^2.

questions.question: Value ₹2000 depreciates 5% yearly for 2 years.
Which grows faster: population at 5% yearly or linear increase?
questions.hint: Percentage-based growth compounds over time.

questions.question: Does cost increase with number of items?
questions.hint: Think about buying more chocolates; will the total price go up?

questions.question: Does time decrease with increased speed?
questions.hint: If you drive faster to the same destination, do you arrive sooner?

questions.question: Does height always increase with age?
questions.hint: Does a 50-year-old keep growing taller than they were at 25?

questions.question: Do all increasing quantities show direct proportion?
questions.hint: Direct proportion requires a constant ratio, not just both going up.

questions.question: What changes when one variable affects another?
questions.hint: The definition of a relationship between variables involves change.

questions.question: If 1 pen costs ₹10, find cost of 6 pens.
questions.hint: Multiply the unit cost by the number of pens.

questions.question: If 5 kg costs ₹100, cost of 15 kg?
questions.hint: 15kg is 3 times 5kg, so multiply cost by 3.

questions.question: Train covers 60 km in 1 hour. Distance in 3 hours?
questions.hint: Multiply speed (60km/h) by time (3h).

questions.question: Cost of 2 items ₹50, cost of 10 items?
questions.hint: Calculate cost for 1 item, then multiply by 10.

questions.question: If petrol doubles, what happens to distance?
questions.hint: Distance and fuel are directly proportional.

questions.question: If x/y is constant, are they directly proportional?
questions.hint: This is the mathematical definition of direct proportion.

questions.question: If x increases but ratio changes, direct proportion?
questions.hint: The ratio x/y must remain constant for direct proportion.

questions.question: Is time and age directly proportional?
questions.hint: Ratio of age to time changes as you get older.

questions.question: Is cost and quantity directly proportional?
questions.hint: Assuming fixed unit price, more items always cost more proportionally.

questions.question: Does doubling x always double y in direct proportion?
questions.hint: y = kx, so if x becomes 2x, y becomes 2y.

questions.question: 1 cm = 5 km. Find actual distance for 4 cm.
questions.hint: Multiply map distance by the scale factor.

questions.question: 5 m costs ₹200. Cost of 20 m?
questions.hint: 20m is 4 times 5m, so the price also quadruples.

questions.question: Height 10 m gives shadow 5 m. Shadow for 20 m?
questions.hint: Object height and shadow length are directly proportional.

questions.question: Map scale 1:1000, 2 cm → actual?
questions.hint: Multiply 2 by the denominator of the scale ratio.

questions.question: Train covers 100 km in 2 hrs. Distance in 6 hrs?
questions.hint: 6 hours is 3 times 2 hours, so distance is tripled.

questions.question: If workers double, what happens to time?
questions.hint: More labor means a task finishes faster.

questions.question: If speed doubles, what happens to time?
questions.hint: Faster speed results in less time to cover the same distance.

questions.question: If x increases 3 times, y becomes?
questions.hint: In inverse proportion, y = k / x.

questions.question: If xy is constant, relation type?
questions.hint: This is the mathematical definition of inverse proportion.

questions.question: Do inverse quantities move in same direction?
questions.hint: One goes up while the other goes down.

questions.question: If xy = constant, are they inversely proportional?
questions.hint: Constant product equals inverse relationship.

questions.question: If product changes, inverse proportion?
questions.hint: Inverse proportion requires the product x * y to stay same.

questions.question: Speed and time for fixed distance relation?
questions.hint: Speed * Time = Distance (constant).

questions.question: Price and number of items (fixed money)?
questions.hint: Price * Quantity = Budget (constant).

questions.question: Height and weight inverse?
questions.hint: There is no constant product relationship between these two.

questions.question: 10 workers finish work in 6 days. Days for 5 workers?
questions.hint: Calculate total work (10 * 6 = 60), then divide by 5.

questions.question: 100 students have food for 20 days. Days for 200?
questions.hint: Double the students means food lasts half as long.

questions.question: 8 pipes fill tank in 4 hrs. Time for 16 pipes?
questions.hint: Double the pipes, half the time.

questions.question: 20 workers → 10 days. Workers for 5 days?
questions.hint: Half the days, double the workers.

questions.question: Car takes 2 hrs at 60 km/h. Time at 120 km/h?
questions.hint: Double the speed, half the time.
